important.subject: Re: Request for Earlier Cloud Billing Visibility
important.category_reason: Active internal thread requiring awareness or response
important.body:
Hi All,

Microsoft Azure invoices have been released as of March 15th. Future Azure invoices will be released on the 15th of the month when possible.

@Jorge Quintero Azure GP batches will be following the format AZUREMMYYYY (e.g. AZURE032026) to differentiate them from MO365 services

MO365 invoices will still be released at the end of the month.

Regards,

Brad Wong

Billing Solutions and Process Analyst

+1 (604) 909-6387
aptum.com | LinkedIn
important.thread_history:
From: Brad Wong <Brad.Wong@aptum.com>

To: Matthew Carter <Matthew.Carter@aptum.com>; Adam Nelson <Adam.Nelson@aptum.com>; Andy Petterson <Andy.Petterson@aptum.com>; Jorge Quintero <Jorge.Quintero@aptum.com>
Cc: Eric Lafreniere <Eric.Lafreniere@aptum.com>; Bryan Gough <Bryan.Gough@aptum.com>; Marc Pare <Marc.Pare@aptum.com>; Will Stevens <Will.Stevens@aptum.com>
Subject: Re: Request for Earlier Cloud Billing Visibility

Sounds good Matt.

I'll coordinate with Tim and Andy to see if we can align our release times to mid-month for the Azure invoices.

Regards,

Brad Wong

Billing Solutions and Process Analyst

+1 (604) 909-6387
aptum.com | LinkedIn

From: Matthew Carter <Matthew.Carter@aptum.com>
Sent: Wednesday, February 25, 2026 8:56 AM

Thank you, @Adam Nelson.

Hi @Brad Wong, let’s move forward with reducing Azure cycles.

As an aside, we’re looking at changing our AWS aggregator and will inquire about their billing cycle.

Thanks,

Matthew Carter

VP, Project Management & Global Supply Chain

+1 (416) 847-0852
aptum.com | LinkedIn

Good point, noted. Let me think about how we go about this.

Adam Nelson

Senior Director, Service Delivery

+1 (438) 802-9796
aptum.com | LinkedIn

From: Matthew Carter <Matthew.Carter@aptum.com>
Sent: February 24, 2026 17:09
To: Brad Wong <Brad.Wong@aptum.com>; Andy Petterson <Andy.Petterson@aptum.com>; Adam Nelson <Adam.Nelson@aptum.com>; Jorge Quintero <Jorge.Quintero@aptum.com>
Cc: Eric Lafreniere <Eric.Lafreniere@aptum.com>; Bryan Gough <Bryan.Gough@aptum.com>; Marc Pare <Marc.Pare@aptum.com>; Will Stevens <Will.Stevens@aptum.com>
Subject: RE: Request for Earlier Cloud Billing Visibility

Thank you, Andy and Brad. I’m of the opinion that we invoice earlier as we’re experiencing challenges. Hopefully, this doesn’t add work to anyone’s plate.

Hi @Adam Nelson,

As you continue your analysis, we should consider shorter net terms especially with customers in higher-risk regions (e.g., we are presently managing a situation with a customer in Mexico where payment has not been received, and our legal counsel has advised that pursuing collection may be complex and resource intensive…)

Matthew Carter

VP, Project Management & Global Supply Chain

+1 (416) 847-0852
aptum.com | LinkedIn

From: Brad Wong <Brad.Wong@aptum.com>
Sent: February 24, 2026 12:37 PM
To: Andy Petterson <Andy.Petterson@aptum.com>; Adam Nelson <Adam.Nelson@aptum.com>; Matthew Carter <Matthew.Carter@aptum.com>; Jorge Quintero <Jorge.Quintero@aptum.com>
Cc: Eric Lafreniere <Eric.Lafreniere@aptum.com>; Bryan Gough <Bryan.Gough@aptum.com>; Marc Pare <Marc.Pare@aptum.com>; Will Stevens <Will.Stevens@aptum.com>
Subject: Re: Request for Earlier Cloud Billing Visibility

Hi All,

To add to Andy, with the current checks I have, I should be able to process the Azure invoices within days of receiving them from Tim's billing engine.

Peeking into my mailbox, the invoices are released sometime between the 11th - 22nd of the month from the billing engine. I'm guessing this is due to reasons Andy has mentioned below, but if we were able to get these out consistently by the 11th or 12th. I could have them posted mid-month.

Needless to say, there will still be the risk of having to retroactively correct orders based on late Ocean orders / sales contracts.

Regards,

Brad Wong

Billing Solutions and Process Analyst

+1 (604) 909-6387
aptum.com | LinkedIn

From: Andy Petterson <Andy.Petterson@aptum.com>
Sent: Tuesday, February 24, 2026 6:42 AM
To: Adam Nelson <Adam.Nelson@aptum.com>; Matthew Carter <Matthew.Carter@aptum.com>; Jorge Quintero <Jorge.Quintero@aptum.com>
Cc: Eric Lafreniere <Eric.Lafreniere@aptum.com>; Bryan Gough <Bryan.Gough@aptum.com>; Marc Pare <Marc.Pare@aptum.com>; Brad Wong <Brad.Wong@aptum.com>; Will Stevens <Will.Stevens@aptum.com>
Subject: Re: Request for Earlier Cloud Billing Visibility

Hi All,

I can't speak to AWS, but for Azure we get the billing data from Microsoft on the 5th-8th of the month for the previous consumption period (so for January usage we'd get the billing data on 5th-8th of February).

Brad then processes that data and issues the invoices near the end of the month eg around 29th. This also gives us time to sort out billing errors or issues, such as a new customer not being added to Ocean correctly.

Not sure how much quicker we could process these, maybe shave a couple weeks off but not much more and if one customer is missing the billing system will refuse to process the whole batch until rectified.

Regards,

Andy Petterson

Solutions Architect

+442380926091
aptum.com | LinkedIn

From: Adam Nelson <Adam.Nelson@aptum.com>
Sent: 24 February 2026 14:40
To: Matthew Carter <Matthew.Carter@aptum.com>; Andy Petterson <Andy.Petterson@aptum.com>; Jorge Quintero <Jorge.Quintero@aptum.com>
Cc: Eric Lafreniere <Eric.Lafreniere@aptum.com>; Bryan Gough <Bryan.Gough@aptum.com>; Marc Pare <Marc.Pare@aptum.com>; Brad Wong <Brad.Wong@aptum.com>; Will Stevens <Will.Stevens@aptum.com>
Subject: Re: Request for Earlier Cloud Billing Visibility

How timely 🙂. I was just peeling this onion.

I'm going to loop in @Jorge Quintero on this one. (and cc Brad...and Will for visibility into potential CoSW needs)

From my understanding "Azure Billing" (https://azbillingapptor01.corp.peer1.net/regions/US/invoices) is the "orchestrator" of these invoices.

I'm not sure how long AFTER month end these get created on our side.

Jorge, can you dig into this and see how quickly we can get invoices?

Matt, from my perspective the "net" is a business decision. I'm ok with a reduced Net on these based on the monetary risk we carry for customers who wait to pay.

Adam Nelson

Senior Director, Service Delivery

+1 (438) 802-9796
aptum.com | LinkedIn

From: Matthew Carter <Matthew.Carter@aptum.com>
Sent: February 24, 2026 09:28
To: Andy Petterson <Andy.Petterson@aptum.com>; Adam Nelson <Adam.Nelson@aptum.com>
Cc: Eric Lafreniere <Eric.Lafreniere@aptum.com>; Bryan Gough <Bryan.Gough@aptum.com>; Marc Pare <Marc.Pare@aptum.com>
Subject: Request for Earlier Cloud Billing Visibility

Hi Adam and Andy,

I’d like to reduce financial exposure related to Azure and AWS billing. Receiving invoices on the 25th of the following month creates risk, particularly if a customer does not pay. The delay leaves us 4-5 months exposed before we identify a problem.

Is there a way to access invoices or preliminary data earlier in the cycle? From a product perspective, could we make these payable in Net 10 vs. Net 30?

Matthew Carter

VP, Project Management & Global Supply Chain

+1 (416) 847-0852
aptum.com | LinkedIn

llm_summary.summary: Brad Wong confirms Azure invoices are now being released on the 15th of each month (first one released March 15th) to improve billing visibility. Azure GP batches will use format AZUREMMYYYY to differentiate from MO365 services, which will continue to be invoiced at month-end. This change was requested by Matthew Carter to address cash flow challenges with certain customers.
tracked_actions: Brad Wong has implemented mid-month Azure invoice release (15th of month)
Jorge Quintero will receive Azure GP batches in new format AZUREMMYYYY
Matthew Carter is evaluating AWS aggregator change and will inquire about billing cycles

important.subject: Re: Smart - Scale - Ticket 12028 - Smart Scale - SQL Server License Verification
important.category_reason: Active internal thread requiring awareness or response
important.body:
To my understanding - Aptum will be held liable and fined if we are audited by Microsoft.

I’m not aware of any ‘abuse’ to be reported except to the client which has already been done.

Barbara Krzywoszanski

Service Delivery Manager

+1 (514) 673-7542
aptum.com | LinkedIn
important.thread_history:
From: Frederic Gingras <Frederic.Gingras@aptum.com>
Date: Thursday, March 12, 2026 at 2:27 PM
To: Elizabeth Perez <Elizabeth.Perez@aptum.com>, Barbara Krzywoszanski <Barbara.Krzywoszanski@aptum.com>, Adam Nelson <Adam.Nelson@aptum.com>, Lacie Allen-Morley <Lacie.Allen-Morley@aptum.com>, Charles Rutledge <Charles.Rutledge@aptum.com>
Subject: Re: Smart - Scale - Ticket 12028 - Smart Scale - SQL Server License Verification

This has not been updated in the ticket.

I had no clue we had an Abuse "department".

Who can confirm if this has been sent to Abuse? As I would expect this to be shown in the ticket, I would assume not. But how can we confirm?

Frederic Gingras

Senior Director, Account Management

m: +1 514-913-9153 teams: +1 514-673-7553
aptum.com | LinkedIn

From: Elizabeth Perez <Elizabeth.Perez@aptum.com>
Sent: March 12, 2026 1:52 PM
To: Frederic Gingras <Frederic.Gingras@aptum.com>; Barbara Krzywoszanski <Barbara.Krzywoszanski@aptum.com>; Adam Nelson <Adam.Nelson@aptum.com>; Lacie Allen-Morley <Lacie.Allen-Morley@aptum.com>; Charles Rutledge <Charles.Rutledge@aptum.com>
Subject: Re: Smart - Scale - Ticket 12028 - Smart Scale - SQL Server License Verification

Hi Fred,

Yes, it's been lingering for quite some time. Somehow momentum was lost back in May when this issue was being discussed with the customer.

@Charles Rutledge indicated that this was going to be reported to Abuse. Not sure what, if any, response was received from that department.

The customer is currently ~11.5 months into a 3‑year contract for SID 7930701, which is one of the services in violation. Aside from purchasing the per‑core SQL licensing from us, they can also procure it directly from Microsoft, provided they share the invoice so we can verify Software Assurance. I agree that Azure may be worth exploring as a more creative alternative, but would need to be evaluate in the context of their existing contract term and whether it meaningfully resolves both the compliance issue and their cost constraints.

Elizabeth Perez

Inside Account Manager

+1 (210) 441-4686
aptum.com | LinkedIn

From: Frederic Gingras <Frederic.Gingras@aptum.com>
Sent: Wednesday, March 11, 2026 5:59 PM
To: Barbara Krzywoszanski <Barbara.Krzywoszanski@aptum.com>; Adam Nelson <Adam.Nelson@aptum.com>; Lacie Allen-Morley <Lacie.Allen-Morley@aptum.com>; Elizabeth Perez <Elizabeth.Perez@aptum.com>
Subject: Re: Smart - Scale - Ticket 12028 - Smart Scale - SQL Server License Verification

Whoah, this has been dragging for almost 1 year!?!

I assume we never sent this to Abuse?

Wondering... would moving them to Azure be an alternative ?

I mean, up to now, what we seem to have provided as alternative, is giving them the cost of the license, which is quite prohibitive for them. But then again, are there other alternatives?

I think we can bring value by being creative on this....

Thoughts?

Frederic Gingras

Senior Director, Account Management

Hello,

Here’s some comms that Jason shared with me about the Smart Cart SQL situation.

@Elizabeth Perez - It’s the same email I sent you yesterday.

Thanks - Barb

Service Delivery Manager

llm_summary.one_line: Aptum faces Microsoft audit liability risk from client's unlicensed SQL Server usage on ticket 12028.
llm_summary.summary: A Smart Scale client has been running SQL Server without proper licensing for nearly a year, creating potential audit liability and fines for Aptum from Microsoft. The issue has stalled since May 2026 despite client notification. The client is 11.5 months into a 3-year contract and has not purchased required per-core licensing or provided proof of direct Microsoft licensing with Software Assurance.
my_actions: Decide on escalation path for SQL Server licensing compliance issue with Smart Scale client (ticket 12028)
Evaluate whether Azure migration is a viable alternative to resolve licensing violation and cost constraints
tracked_actions: Team discussing whether issue was reported to Abuse department (no confirmation in ticket)
Elizabeth Perez and Frederic Gingras exploring licensing alternatives with client
llm_summary.suggested_reply: Thanks for flagging this compliance risk. We need to resolve this immediately given the audit liability. Fred, let's schedule a call today to align on next steps — whether that's enforcing compliance, exploring Azure migration, or escalating through our standard abuse process. Barbara, please document all client communications in the ticket and confirm our contractual position if we need to suspend service.

important.subject: Re: Smart - Scale - Ticket 12028 - Smart Scale - SQL Server License Verification
important.category_reason: Active internal thread requiring awareness or response
important.body:
When the previous manager of Abuse (Joseph) was exited last year, a lot of historical knowledge was lost. It's also worth clarifying that he was a 1-man team; he had no reports. His Abuse responsibilities were given to InfoSec to handle as best we can, and since then, we have been trying to document some of that knowledge based on our interpretation of what Abuse should handle, which does align with what Adam said: the mis-use of our services.

I don't believe license (mis-)use should fall under the purview of Abuse. If a customer has installed or is using software and violating the license agreement, should Aptum be policing that at all? If we misinterpret a license agreement, couldn't that make Aptum liable? (I'm not saying Charles has misinterpreted it in this ticket, more as a general thought).

This ticket likely slipped through the cracks during the transition periods mentioned above, and also because, like IT, the InfoSec team don't typically look at the "Aptum" project in Jira - we have dedicated Jira projects for Security work.

Cheers,

Fred

Fred Taylor-Young

Senior Manager, IT & Security

llm_summary.one_line: InfoSec clarifies license violations shouldn't fall under Abuse; historical knowledge lost when Joseph exited.
llm_summary.summary: Fred Taylor-Young explains that when the previous Abuse manager (Joseph) left last year, his responsibilities transferred to InfoSec, but they don't believe software license violations should be their purview. He notes the ticket (12028) likely fell through the cracks during the transition and raises liability concerns about Aptum policing customer license agreements. This responds to Adam's earlier question about whether this SQL Server license issue belongs with the Abuse team.
my_actions: Decide who should own customer software license compliance issues going forward
Determine if ticket 12028 needs reassignment or closure
tracked_actions: Fred Taylor-Young and InfoSec team are documenting Abuse responsibilities after Joseph's exit
Team is clarifying whether license violations fall under Abuse scope
llm_summary.suggested_reply: Thanks Fred. You're right that license policing creates liability risk for us. Let's close the loop on ticket 12028 — can you confirm the current status and whether we need to reassign it? We should also document the boundary between Abuse (service misuse) and license compliance so this doesn't happen again.

HI Marc,

Please find below the answers to your questions.

We have clients that are using AWS native tools for cost reporting and we are keen to understand when/what changes with their experience.

From your customer’s perspective, there should be no change to their experience when it comes to using AWS native cost reporting tools. Services such as Cost Explorer, CUR (Cost and Usage Reports), and other AWS-native billing and reporting capabilities will continue to function exactly as they do today.The transition to TD SYNNEX as the distribution partner primarily affects the billing and distribution relationship on the backend, not the operational environment within AWS.

We are wondering if there is a two hop route to implementing this change and what the impact would be to our margin. Phase 1) just make TD our distribution , Phase 2) move some customers over to new model that removes Root for Distribution.

Now, there isn’t a simple or automatic way to transfer accounts between different distribution models. While initiating the transition to TD SYNNEX as the distributor is certainly possible, moving accounts across structural models typically requires careful coordination and review on a case-by-case basis to ensure the transition is handled smoothly.

I would also recommend reviewing the exit clause with your current distributor. In most cases, partners are required to provide a 90-day notice period before making a move to another distributor. Ensuring this is aligned early in the process helps avoid any delays or contractual complications during the transition.

Kindly revert on any clarifications.

Thanks & Regards

Rupesh Sankar

Business Development Manager

Subject: RE: AWS Cloud Marketplace

Hi Christine, Gina & Rupesh

Please treat as priority and address comments in blue:

Custom cost-management tools and Security dashboards via StreamOne Ion?

Yes, the StreamOne platform provides pre-built dashboards and the ability to create custom reports, including configurable alerts. These reports can show usage, consumption trends, and other financial insights.

StreamOne also offers real-time reporting on IaaS consumption (Azure, AWS, and Google), enabling advanced cost-optimization capabilities and continuous billing visibility, not just month-end reporting.

In addition, StreamOne includes a SecOps Portal for partners, providing a centralized dashboard to view and manage customer security features and policies.

If you need more specific details, I’m happy to follow up.

Please provide detailed steps how you will assist us with the change-over from another aggregator to TDS?

Access to AWS funding programs, ACE deal registrations and leads?

Yes, As a Premier Tier AWS Partner, we help our partners leverage AWS mechanisms and programs, including offerings, funding, enablement, and guidance on ACE deal registrations as part of co-sell execution (where eligible and aligned to AWS criteria).

The TD SYNNEX AWS team also has a dedicated Customer Success and Conversion team focused on customer lead generation and demand generation. Who exactly would be part of our team? Where does Gina fit into the picture and how can we utilize her as a resource?

We’ve built an infrastructure framework around programs like SBAI and other third-party lead-gen mechanisms to maintain, hydrate, nurture, and route end-customer leads to our partners—across both Commercial and Public Sector.

API access or automated insights for billing data independent of AWS (i.e., aligning payment terms, managing outstanding balances are important to us)

Yes, both are available. I can connect you with the team that managed this process so they can provide a detailed overview and answer any specific questions.

What GTM strategies do you offer? Free training and discounted workshops? Co-sponsored AWS events?

Yes, TD SYNNEX offers several GTM motions to help partners build and scale pipeline, including enablement, co-marketing, and co-sell support.

Pipeline: Top-of-funnel, assessment-led sales plays for customers both on AWS and not yet on AWS.
Training: Fully subsidized training and enablement programs are available.
Events: Depending on the use case, eligibility, and cost, we can either fully subsidize or co-invest in joint webinars, SKOs, and customer-facing events—provided there’s a defined solution focus, target audience, pipeline target, and measurable ROI.

On average, how much MDF credits can we anticipate receiving monthly/annually from the TDSynnex program? We would like to see details as this is important to us.

Will we be assigned a dedicated account manager, technical expert or help with pre-sales and design? (saving strategies and rightsizing recommendations are critical).

Yes, you’ll have a dedicated AWS account manager, plus access to technical SMEs and pre-sales engineering resources. Again, who would be part of our team, Gina?

Given the breadth of AWS service delivery designations and competencies (e.g., MSP, migrations, government, education, to name a few), our AWS practice provides technical expertise and support across migrations & modernization, managed services, professional services, assessments, and AWS workloads.

Additionally, our ServiceSolv team provides engineering, integration, deployment, and remediation services which enables you to offer end-to-end customer lifecycle support without building these functions internally. We can also provide white-labeled resources delivered under your branding, where applicable.

Net 90 terms

Extended payment terms are feasible through TD SYNNEX. We'd have to get you in contact with our credit/finance team to review your eligibility If 90 is denied for whatever reason, what is your standard, 60? 2% Net 10?

If Aptum has an AWS customer that goes bankrupt or denies us payment, what programs do you have to reduce this burden? Historically, we’ve had to pay for AWS consumption and cannot collect from a bankrupt customer…

Besides 10th of the following month billing; what other sweeteners can you add to incentivize Aptum to award TDSynnex over a competitor?

Key Discussion Points

Current Situation

Customer considering going direct to AWS without better pricing

April 1st AWS Program Changes

Major structural changes:

Partners receive full 8% front-end discount (up from 3%)

Distributors move to back-end rebate only (no front-end discount)

New billing transfer program creates visibility silos between customer/partner/distributor

TDS Model:

100% pass-through: "You get what you earn"

No tiered programs—partners receive full earned discounts

Root exception & billing transfer capabilities ready now

Additional Opportunity: Fintech Customer Migration

Current spend: $200K+/month direct with AWS

Aptum led migration from $0 → $50K+/month using MAP funding

Opportunity: TDS can build exception case based on historical migration work and APFP submissions

Billing transfer allows account capture while customer retains root access

Hello Aptum team,

Nice to e-meet you all.

Thank you for your due diligence, you’re asking all the right questions. I did my best to respond succinctly in email, but I’d love to walk through each point in more depth on a call, especially if you’re interested in how we differentiate compared to other AWS distributors.

Custom cost-management tools and Security dashboards via StreamOne Ion?

Yes, the StreamOne platform provides pre-built dashboards and the ability to create custom reports, including configurable alerts. These reports can show usage, consumption trends, and other financial insights.

StreamOne also offers real-time reporting on IaaS consumption (Azure, AWS, and Google), enabling advanced cost-optimization capabilities and continuous billing visibility, not just month-end reporting.

In addition, StreamOne includes a SecOps Portal for partners, providing a centralized dashboard to view and manage customer security features and policies.

If you need more specific details, I’m happy to follow up.

Access to AWS funding programs, ACE deal registrations and leads?

Yes, As a Premier Tier AWS Partner, we help our partners leverage AWS mechanisms and programs, including offerings, funding, enablement, and guidance on ACE deal registrations as part of co-sell execution (where eligible and aligned to AWS criteria).

The TD SYNNEX AWS team also has a dedicated Customer Success and Conversion team focused on customer lead generation and demand generation.

We’ve built an infrastructure framework around programs like SBAI and other third-party lead-gen mechanisms to maintain, hydrate, nurture, and route end-customer leads to our partners—across both Commercial and Public Sector.

API access or automated insights for billing data independent of AWS (i.e., aligning payment terms, managing outstanding balances are important to us)

Yes, both are available. I can connect you with the team that managed this process so they can provide a detailed overview and answer any specific questions.

What GTM strategies do you offer? Free training and discounted workshops? Co-sponsored AWS events?

Yes, TD SYNNEX offers several GTM motions to help partners build and scale pipeline, including enablement, co-marketing, and co-sell support.

Pipeline: Top-of-funnel, assessment-led sales plays for customers both on AWS and not yet on AWS.
Training: Fully subsidized training and enablement programs are available.
Events: Depending on the use case, eligibility, and cost, we can either fully subsidize or co-invest in joint webinars, SKOs, and customer-facing events—provided there’s a defined solution focus, target audience, pipeline target, and measurable ROI.

Will we be assigned a dedicated account manager, technical expert or help with pre-sales and design? (saving strategies and rightsizing recommendations are critical).

Yes, you’ll have a dedicated AWS account manager, plus access to technical SMEs and pre-sales engineering resources.

Given the breadth of AWS service delivery designations and competencies (e.g., MSP, migrations, government, education, to name a few), our AWS practice provides technical expertise and support across migrations & modernization, managed services, professional services, assessments, and AWS workloads.

Additionally, our ServiceSolv team provides engineering, integration, deployment, and remediation services which enables you to offer end-to-end customer lifecycle support without building these functions internally. We can also provide white-labeled resources delivered under your branding, where applicable.

Net 90 terms

Extended payment terms are feasible through TD SYNNEX. We'd have to get you in contact with our credit/finance team to review your eligibility

Thanks,

Michael

Yes, the StreamOne platform provides pre-built dashboards and the ability to create custom reports, including configurable alerts. These reports can show usage, consumption trends, and other financial insights.

StreamOne also offers real-time reporting on IaaS consumption (Azure, AWS, and Google), enabling advanced cost-optimization capabilities and continuous billing visibility, not just month-end reporting.

In addition, StreamOne includes a SecOps Portal for partners, providing a centralized dashboard to view and manage customer security features and policies.

Yes, both are available. I can connect you with the team that managed this process so they can provide a detailed overview and answer any specific questions.

Yes, TD SYNNEX offers several GTM motions to help partners build and scale pipeline, including enablement, co-marketing, and co-sell support.

Pipeline: Top-of-funnel, assessment-led sales plays for customers both on AWS and not yet on AWS.
Training: Fully subsidized training and enablement programs are available.

Hello Aptum team,

Nice to e-meet you all.

Thank you for your due diligence, you’re asking all the right questions. I did my best to respond succinctly in email, but I’d love to walk through each point in more depth on a call, especially if you’re interested in how we differentiate compared to other AWS distributors.

Custom cost-management tools and Security dashboards via StreamOne Ion?

Yes, the StreamOne platform provides pre-built dashboards and the ability to create custom reports, including configurable alerts. These reports can show usage, consumption trends, and other financial insights.

StreamOne also offers real-time reporting on IaaS consumption (Azure, AWS, and Google), enabling advanced cost-optimization capabilities and continuous billing visibility, not just month-end reporting.

In addition, StreamOne includes a SecOps Portal for partners, providing a centralized dashboard to view and manage customer security features and policies.

If you need more specific details, I’m happy to follow up.

Access to AWS funding programs, ACE deal registrations and leads?

Yes, As a Premier Tier AWS Partner, we help our partners leverage AWS mechanisms and programs, including offerings, funding, enablement, and guidance on ACE deal registrations as part of co-sell execution (where eligible and aligned to AWS criteria).

The TD SYNNEX AWS team also has a dedicated Customer Success and Conversion team focused on customer lead generation and demand generation.

We’ve built an infrastructure framework around programs like SBAI and other third-party lead-gen mechanisms to maintain, hydrate, nurture, and route end-customer leads to our partners—across both Commercial and Public Sector.

API access or automated insights for billing data independent of AWS (i.e., aligning payment terms, managing outstanding balances are important to us)

Yes, both are available. I can connect you with the team that managed this process so they can provide a detailed overview and answer any specific questions.

What GTM strategies do you offer? Free training and discounted workshops? Co-sponsored AWS events?

Yes, TD SYNNEX offers several GTM motions to help partners build and scale pipeline, including enablement, co-marketing, and co-sell support.

Pipeline: Top-of-funnel, assessment-led sales plays for customers both on AWS and not yet on AWS.
Training: Fully subsidized training and enablement programs are available.
Events: Depending on the use case, eligibility, and cost, we can either fully subsidize or co-invest in joint webinars, SKOs, and customer-facing events—provided there’s a defined solution focus, target audience, pipeline target, and measurable ROI.

Will we be assigned a dedicated account manager, technical expert or help with pre-sales and design? (saving strategies and rightsizing recommendations are critical).

Yes, you’ll have a dedicated AWS account manager, plus access to technical SMEs and pre-sales engineering resources.

Given the breadth of AWS service delivery designations and competencies (e.g., MSP, migrations, government, education, to name a few), our AWS practice provides technical expertise and support across migrations & modernization, managed services, professional services, assessments, and AWS workloads.

Additionally, our ServiceSolv team provides engineering, integration, deployment, and remediation services which enables you to offer end-to-end customer lifecycle support without building these functions internally. We can also provide white-labeled resources delivered under your branding, where applicable.

Net 90 terms

Extended payment terms are feasible through TD SYNNEX. We'd have to get you in contact with our credit/finance team to review your eligibility

Thanks,

Michael

Michael Campbell

Manager, AWS Commercial and Public Sector Sales

llm_summary.one_line: Adam votes to proceed with TD Synnex as AWS supplier and wants Andrei and Lacie looped in for customer transition.
llm_summary.summary: Adam has confirmed his vote to move forward with TD Synnex (TDS) as the AWS Cloud Marketplace supplier for the U.S. region. Matthew Carter is awaiting final consensus and a transition date, noting they may miss the April 1 deadline which could impact Basis financially. Adam wants to ensure Andrei and Lacie are involved in handling customer communications, meetings, and implementation once the transition begins.

Please treat as priority and address comments in blue:

Custom cost-management tools and Security dashboards via StreamOne Ion?

Yes, the StreamOne platform provides pre-built dashboards and the ability to create custom reports, including configurable alerts. These reports can show usage, consumption trends, and other financial insights.

StreamOne also offers real-time reporting on IaaS consumption (Azure, AWS, and Google), enabling advanced cost-optimization capabilities and continuous billing visibility, not just month-end reporting.

In addition, StreamOne includes a SecOps Portal for partners, providing a centralized dashboard to view and manage customer security features and policies.

If you need more specific details, I’m happy to follow up.

Please provide detailed steps how you will assist us with the change-over from another aggregator to TDS?

Access to AWS funding programs, ACE deal registrations and leads?

Yes, As a Premier Tier AWS Partner, we help our partners leverage AWS mechanisms and programs, including offerings, funding, enablement, and guidance on ACE deal registrations as part of co-sell execution (where eligible and aligned to AWS criteria).

The TD SYNNEX AWS team also has a dedicated Customer Success and Conversion team focused on customer lead generation and demand generation. Who exactly would be part of our team? Where does Gina fit into the picture and how can we utilize her as a resource?

We’ve built an infrastructure framework around programs like SBAI and other third-party lead-gen mechanisms to maintain, hydrate, nurture, and route end-customer leads to our partners—across both Commercial and Public Sector.

API access or automated insights for billing data independent of AWS (i.e., aligning payment terms, managing outstanding balances are important to us)

Yes, both are available. I can connect you with the team that managed this process so they can provide a detailed overview and answer any specific questions.

What GTM strategies do you offer? Free training and discounted workshops? Co-sponsored AWS events?

Yes, TD SYNNEX offers several GTM motions to help partners build and scale pipeline, including enablement, co-marketing, and co-sell support.

Pipeline: Top-of-funnel, assessment-led sales plays for customers both on AWS and not yet on AWS.
Training: Fully subsidized training and enablement programs are available.
Events: Depending on the use case, eligibility, and cost, we can either fully subsidize or co-invest in joint webinars, SKOs, and customer-facing events—provided there’s a defined solution focus, target audience, pipeline target, and measurable ROI.

Yes, you’ll have a dedicated AWS account manager, plus access to technical SMEs and pre-sales engineering resources. Again, who would be part of our team, Gina?

Given the breadth of AWS service delivery designations and competencies (e.g., MSP, migrations, government, education, to name a few), our AWS practice provides technical expertise and support across migrations & modernization, managed services, professional services, assessments, and AWS workloads.

Additionally, our ServiceSolv team provides engineering, integration, deployment, and remediation services which enables you to offer end-to-end customer lifecycle support without building these functions internally. Hello Aptum team,

Nice to e-meet you all.

Thank you for your due diligence, you’re asking all the right questions. I did my best to respond succinctly in email, but I’d love to walk through each point in more depth on a call, especially if you’re interested in how we differentiate compared to other AWS distributors.

Custom cost-management tools and Security dashboards via StreamOne Ion?

Yes, the StreamOne platform provides pre-built dashboards and the ability to create custom reports, including configurable alerts. These reports can show usage, consumption trends, and other financial insights.

StreamOne also offers real-time reporting on IaaS consumption (Azure, AWS, and Google), enabling advanced cost-optimization capabilities and continuous billing visibility, not just month-end reporting.

In addition, StreamOne includes a SecOps Portal for partners, providing a centralized dashboard to view and manage customer security features and policies.

If you need more specific details, I’m happy to follow up.

Access to AWS funding programs, ACE deal registrations and leads?

Yes, As a Premier Tier AWS Partner, we help our partners leverage AWS mechanisms and programs, including offerings, funding, enablement, and guidance on ACE deal registrations as part of co-sell execution (where eligible and aligned to AWS criteria).

The TD SYNNEX AWS team also has a dedicated Customer Success and Conversion team focused on customer lead generation and demand generation.

We’ve built an infrastructure framework around programs like SBAI and other third-party lead-gen mechanisms to maintain, hydrate, nurture, and route end-customer leads to our partners—across both Commercial and Public Sector.

API access or automated insights for billing data independent of AWS (i.e., aligning payment terms, managing outstanding balances are important to us)

Yes, both are available. I can connect you with the team that managed this process so they can provide a detailed overview and answer any specific questions.

What GTM strategies do you offer? Free training and discounted workshops? Co-sponsored AWS events?

Yes, TD SYNNEX offers several GTM motions to help partners build and scale pipeline, including enablement, co-marketing, and co-sell support.

Pipeline: Top-of-funnel, assessment-led sales plays for customers both on AWS and not yet on AWS.
Training: Fully subsidized training and enablement programs are available.
Events: Depending on the use case, eligibility, and cost, we can either fully subsidize or co-invest in joint webinars, SKOs, and customer-facing events—provided there’s a defined solution focus, target audience, pipeline target, and measurable ROI.

Will we be assigned a dedicated account manager, technical expert or help with pre-sales and design? (saving strategies and rightsizing recommendations are critical).

Yes, you’ll have a dedicated AWS account manager, plus access to technical SMEs and pre-sales engineering resources.

Given the breadth of AWS service delivery designations and competencies (e.g., MSP, migrations, government, education, to name a few), our AWS practice provides technical expertise and support across migrations & modernization, managed services, professional services, assessments, and AWS workloads.

Additionally, our ServiceSolv team provides engineering, integration, deployment, and remediation services which enables you to offer end-to-end customer lifecycle support without building these functions internally. We can also provide white-labeled resources delivered under your branding, where applicable.

Net 90 terms

Extended payment terms are feasible through TD SYNNEX. We'd have to get you in contact with our credit/finance team to review your eligibility

Thanks,

Michael

Manager, AWS Commercial and Public Sector Sales

Custom cost-management tools and Security dashboards via StreamOne Ion?

Yes, the StreamOne platform provides pre-built dashboards and the ability to create custom reports, including configurable alerts. These reports can show usage, consumption trends, and other financial insights.

StreamOne also offers real-time reporting on IaaS consumption (Azure, AWS, and Google), enabling advanced cost-optimization capabilities and continuous billing visibility, not just month-end reporting.

In addition, StreamOne includes a SecOps Portal for partners, providing a centralized dashboard to view and manage customer security features and policies.

If you need more specific details, I’m happy to follow up.

Please provide detailed steps how you will assist us with the change-over from another aggregator to TDS?

Access to AWS funding programs, ACE deal registrations and leads?

Yes, As a Premier Tier AWS Partner, we help our partners leverage AWS mechanisms and programs, including offerings, funding, enablement, and guidance on ACE deal registrations as part of co-sell execution (where eligible and aligned to AWS criteria).

The TD SYNNEX AWS team also has a dedicated Customer Success and Conversion team focused on customer lead generation and demand generation. Who exactly would be part of our team? Where does Gina fit into the picture and how can we utilize her as a resource?

We’ve built an infrastructure framework around programs like SBAI and other third-party lead-gen mechanisms to maintain, hydrate, nurture, and route end-customer leads to our partners—across both Commercial and Public Sector.

API access or automated insights for billing data independent of AWS (i.e., aligning payment terms, managing outstanding balances are important to us)

Yes, both are available. I can connect you with the team that managed this process so they can provide a detailed overview and answer any specific questions.

What GTM strategies do you offer? Free training and discounted workshops? Co-sponsored AWS events?

Yes, TD SYNNEX offers several GTM motions to help partners build and scale pipeline, including enablement, co-marketing, and co-sell support.

Pipeline: Top-of-funnel, assessment-led sales plays for customers both on AWS and not yet on AWS.
Training: Fully subsidized training and enablement programs are available.
Events: Depending on the use case, eligibility, and cost, we can either fully subsidize or co-invest in joint webinars, SKOs, and customer-facing events—provided there’s a defined solution focus, target audience, pipeline target, and measurable ROI.

Hello Aptum team,

Nice to e-meet you all.

Thank you for your due diligence, you’re asking all the right questions. I did my best to respond succinctly in email, but I’d love to walk through each point in more depth on a call, especially if you’re interested in how we differentiate compared to other AWS distributors.

Custom cost-management tools and Security dashboards via StreamOne Ion?

Yes, the StreamOne platform provides pre-built dashboards and the ability to create custom reports, including configurable alerts. These reports can show usage, consumption trends, and other financial insights.

StreamOne also offers real-time reporting on IaaS consumption (Azure, AWS, and Google), enabling advanced cost-optimization capabilities and continuous billing visibility, not just month-end reporting.

In addition, StreamOne includes a SecOps Portal for partners, providing a centralized dashboard to view and manage customer security features and policies.

If you need more specific details, I’m happy to follow up.

Access to AWS funding programs, ACE deal registrations and leads?

Yes, As a Premier Tier AWS Partner, we help our partners leverage AWS mechanisms and programs, including offerings, funding, enablement, and guidance on ACE deal registrations as part of co-sell execution (where eligible and aligned to AWS criteria).

The TD SYNNEX AWS team also has a dedicated Customer Success and Conversion team focused on customer lead generation and demand generation.

We’ve built an infrastructure framework around programs like SBAI and other third-party lead-gen mechanisms to maintain, hydrate, nurture, and route end-customer leads to our partners—across both Commercial and Public Sector.

API access or automated insights for billing data independent of AWS (i.e., aligning payment terms, managing outstanding balances are important to us)

Yes, both are available. I can connect you with the team that managed this process so they can provide a detailed overview and answer any specific questions.

What GTM strategies do you offer? Free training and discounted workshops? Co-sponsored AWS events?

Yes, TD SYNNEX offers several GTM motions to help partners build and scale pipeline, including enablement, co-marketing, and co-sell support.

Pipeline: Top-of-funnel, assessment-led sales plays for customers both on AWS and not yet on AWS.
Training: Fully subsidized training and enablement programs are available.
Events: Depending on the use case, eligibility, and cost, we can either fully subsidize or co-invest in joint webinars, SKOs, and customer-facing events—provided there’s a defined solution focus, target audience, pipeline target, and measurable ROI.

Will we be assigned a dedicated account manager, technical expert or help with pre-sales and design? (saving strategies and rightsizing recommendations are critical).

Yes, you’ll have a dedicated AWS account manager, plus access to technical SMEs and pre-sales engineering resources.

Given the breadth of AWS service delivery designations and competencies (e.g., MSP, migrations, government, education, to name a few), our AWS practice provides technical expertise and support across migrations & modernization, managed services, professional services, assessments, and AWS workloads.

Additionally, our ServiceSolv team provides engineering, integration, deployment, and remediation services which enables you to offer end-to-end customer lifecycle support without building these functions internally. We can also provide white-labeled resources delivered under your branding, where applicable.

Net 90 terms

Extended payment terms are feasible through TD SYNNEX. We'd have to get you in contact with our credit/finance team to review your eligibility

Thanks,

Michael

HI Marc,

Please find below the answers to your questions.

We have clients that are using AWS native tools for cost reporting and we are keen to understand when/what changes with their experience.

From your customer’s perspective, there should be no change to their experience when it comes to using AWS native cost reporting tools. Services such as Cost Explorer, CUR (Cost and Usage Reports), and other AWS-native billing and reporting capabilities will continue to function exactly as they do today.The transition to TD SYNNEX as the distribution partner primarily affects the billing and distribution relationship on the backend, not the operational environment within AWS.

We are wondering if there is a two hop route to implementing this change and what the impact would be to our margin. Phase 1) just make TD our distribution , Phase 2) move some customers over to new model that removes Root for Distribution.

Now, there isn’t a simple or automatic way to transfer accounts between different distribution models. While initiating the transition to TD SYNNEX as the distributor is certainly possible, moving accounts across structural models typically requires careful coordination and review on a case-by-case basis to ensure the transition is handled smoothly.

I would also recommend reviewing the exit clause with your current distributor. In most cases, partners are required to provide a 90-day notice period before making a move to another distributor. Ensuring this is aligned early in the process helps avoid any delays or contractual complications during the transition.

Kindly revert on any clarifications.

Thanks & Regards

Rupesh Sankar

Business Development Manager

TD SYNNEX

Mississauga, ON, L5N 8G1

Hi Christine, Gina & Rupesh

Please treat as priority and address comments in blue:

Custom cost-management tools and Security dashboards via StreamOne Ion?

Yes, the StreamOne platform provides pre-built dashboards and the ability to create custom reports, including configurable alerts. These reports can show usage, consumption trends, and other financial insights.

StreamOne also offers real-time reporting on IaaS consumption (Azure, AWS, and Google), enabling advanced cost-optimization capabilities and continuous billing visibility, not just month-end reporting.

In addition, StreamOne includes a SecOps Portal for partners, providing a centralized dashboard to view and manage customer security features and policies.

If you need more specific details, I’m happy to follow up.

Please provide detailed steps how you will assist us with the change-over from another aggregator to TDS?

Access to AWS funding programs, ACE deal registrations and leads?

Yes, As a Premier Tier AWS Partner, we help our partners leverage AWS mechanisms and programs, including offerings, funding, enablement, and guidance on ACE deal registrations as part of co-sell execution (where eligible and aligned to AWS criteria).

The TD SYNNEX AWS team also has a dedicated Customer Success and Conversion team focused on customer lead generation and demand generation. Who exactly would be part of our team? Where does Gina fit into the picture and how can we utilize her as a resource?

We’ve built an infrastructure framework around programs like SBAI and other third-party lead-gen mechanisms to maintain, hydrate, nurture, and route end-customer leads to our partners—across both Commercial and Public Sector.

API access or automated insights for billing data independent of AWS (i.e., aligning payment terms, managing outstanding balances are important to us)

Yes, both are available. I can connect you with the team that managed this process so they can provide a detailed overview and answer any specific questions.

What GTM strategies do you offer? Free training and discounted workshops? Co-sponsored AWS events?

Yes, TD SYNNEX offers several GTM motions to help partners build and scale pipeline, including enablement, co-marketing, and co-sell support.

Pipeline: Top-of-funnel, assessment-led sales plays for customers both on AWS and not yet on AWS.
Training: Fully subsidized training and enablement programs are available.
Events: Depending on the use case, eligibility, and cost, we can either fully subsidize or co-invest in joint webinars, SKOs, and customer-facing events—provided there’s a defined solution focus, target audience, pipeline target, and measurable ROI.

On average, how much MDF credits can we anticipate receiving monthly/annually from the TDSynnex program? We would like to see details as this is important to us.

Will we be assigned a dedicated account manager, technical expert or help with pre-sales and design? (saving strategies and rightsizing recommendations are critical).

Yes, you’ll have a dedicated AWS account manager, plus access to technical SMEs and pre-sales engineering resources. Again, who would be part of our team, Gina?

Given the breadth of AWS service delivery designations and competencies (e.g., MSP, migrations, government, education, to name a few), our AWS practice provides technical expertise and support across migrations & modernization, managed services, professional services, assessments, and AWS workloads.

Additionally, our ServiceSolv team provides engineering, integration, deployment, and remediation services which enables you to offer end-to-end customer lifecycle support without building these functions internally. We can also provide white-labeled resources delivered under your branding, where applicable.

Net 90 terms

Extended payment terms are feasible through TD SYNNEX. We'd have to get you in contact with our credit/finance team to review your eligibility If 90 is denied for whatever reason, what is your standard, 60? 2% Net 10?

Thank you for your due diligence, you’re asking all the right questions. I did my best to respond succinctly in email, but I’d love to walk through each point in more depth on a call, especially if you’re interested in how we differentiate compared to other AWS distributors.

Custom cost-management tools and Security dashboards via StreamOne Ion?

Yes, the StreamOne platform provides pre-built dashboards and the ability to create custom reports, including configurable alerts. These reports can show usage, consumption trends, and other financial insights.

StreamOne also offers real-time reporting on IaaS consumption (Azure, AWS, and Google), enabling advanced cost-optimization capabilities and continuous billing visibility, not just month-end reporting.

In addition, StreamOne includes a SecOps Portal for partners, providing a centralized dashboard to view and manage customer security features and policies.

If you need more specific details, I’m happy to follow up.

Access to AWS funding programs, ACE deal registrations and leads?

Yes, As a Premier Tier AWS Partner, we help our partners leverage AWS mechanisms and programs, including offerings, funding, enablement, and guidance on ACE deal registrations as part of co-sell execution (where eligible and aligned to AWS criteria).

The TD SYNNEX AWS team also has a dedicated Customer Success and Conversion team focused on customer lead generation and demand generation.

We’ve built an infrastructure framework around programs like SBAI and other third-party lead-gen mechanisms to maintain, hydrate, nurture, and route end-customer leads to our partners—across both Commercial and Public Sector.

API access or automated insights for billing data independent of AWS (i.e., aligning payment terms, managing outstanding balances are important to us)

Yes, both are available. I can connect you with the team that managed this process so they can provide a detailed overview and answer any specific questions.

What GTM strategies do you offer? Free training and discounted workshops? Co-sponsored AWS events?

Yes, TD SYNNEX offers several GTM motions to help partners build and scale pipeline, including enablement, co-marketing, and co-sell support.

Pipeline: Top-of-funnel, assessment-led sales plays for customers both on AWS and not yet on AWS.
Training: Fully subsidized training and enablement programs are available.
Events: Depending on the use case, eligibility, and cost, we can either fully subsidize or co-invest in joint webinars, SKOs, and customer-facing events—provided there’s a defined solution focus, target audience, pipeline target, and measurable ROI.

Will we be assigned a dedicated account manager, technical expert or help with pre-sales and design? (saving strategies and rightsizing recommendations are critical).

Yes, you’ll have a dedicated AWS account manager, plus access to technical SMEs and pre-sales engineering resources.

Given the breadth of AWS service delivery designations and competencies (e.g., MSP, migrations, government, education, to name a few), our AWS practice provides technical expertise and support across migrations & modernization, managed services, professional services, assessments, and AWS workloads.

Additionally, our ServiceSolv team provides engineering, integration, deployment, and remediation services which enables you to offer end-to-end customer lifecycle support without building these functions internally. We can also provide white-labeled resources delivered under your branding, where applicable.

Net 90 terms

Extended payment terms are feasible through TD SYNNEX. We'd have to get you in contact with our credit/finance team to review your eligibility

Thanks,

Michael

HI Marc,

Please find below the answers to your questions.

We have clients that are using AWS native tools for cost reporting and we are keen to understand when/what changes with their experience.

From your customer’s perspective, there should be no change to their experience when it comes to using AWS native cost reporting tools. Services such as Cost Explorer, CUR (Cost and Usage Reports), and other AWS-native billing and reporting capabilities will continue to function exactly as they do today.The transition to TD SYNNEX as the distribution partner primarily affects the billing and distribution relationship on the backend, not the operational environment within AWS.

We are wondering if there is a two hop route to implementing this change and what the impact would be to our margin. Phase 1) just make TD our distribution , Phase 2) move some customers over to new model that removes Root for Distribution.

Now, there isn’t a simple or automatic way to transfer accounts between different distribution models. While initiating the transition to TD SYNNEX as the distributor is certainly possible, moving accounts across structural models typically requires careful coordination and review on a case-by-case basis to ensure the transition is handled smoothly.

I would also recommend reviewing the exit clause with your current distributor. In most cases, partners are required to provide a 90-day notice period before making a move to another distributor. Ensuring this is aligned early in the process helps avoid any delays or contractual complications during the transition.

Kindly revert on any clarifications.

Thanks & Regards

Hello Aptum team,

Nice to e-meet you all.

Thank you for your due diligence, you’re asking all the right questions. I did my best to respond succinctly in email, but I’d love to walk through each point in more depth on a call, especially if you’re interested in how we differentiate compared to other AWS distributors.

Custom cost-management tools and Security dashboards via StreamOne Ion?

Yes, the StreamOne platform provides pre-built dashboards and the ability to create custom reports, including configurable alerts. These reports can show usage, consumption trends, and other financial insights.

StreamOne also offers real-time reporting on IaaS consumption (Azure, AWS, and Google), enabling advanced cost-optimization capabilities and continuous billing visibility, not just month-end reporting.

In addition, StreamOne includes a SecOps Portal for partners, providing a centralized dashboard to view and manage customer security features and policies.

If you need more specific details, I’m happy to follow up.

Access to AWS funding programs, ACE deal registrations and leads?

Yes, As a Premier Tier AWS Partner, we help our partners leverage AWS mechanisms and programs, including offerings, funding, enablement, and guidance on ACE deal registrations as part of co-sell execution (where eligible and aligned to AWS criteria).

The TD SYNNEX AWS team also has a dedicated Customer Success and Conversion team focused on customer lead generation and demand generation.

We’ve built an infrastructure framework around programs like SBAI and other third-party lead-gen mechanisms to maintain, hydrate, nurture, and route end-customer leads to our partners—across both Commercial and Public Sector.

API access or automated insights for billing data independent of AWS (i.e., aligning payment terms, managing outstanding balances are important to us)

Yes, both are available. I can connect you with the team that managed this process so they can provide a detailed overview and answer any specific questions.

What GTM strategies do you offer? Free training and discounted workshops? Co-sponsored AWS events?

Yes, TD SYNNEX offers several GTM motions to help partners build and scale pipeline, including enablement, co-marketing, and co-sell support.

Pipeline: Top-of-funnel, assessment-led sales plays for customers both on AWS and not yet on AWS.
Training: Fully subsidized training and enablement programs are available.
Events: Depending on the use case, eligibility, and cost, we can either fully subsidize or co-invest in joint webinars, SKOs, and customer-facing events—provided there’s a defined solution focus, target audience, pipeline target, and measurable ROI.

Will we be assigned a dedicated account manager, technical expert or help with pre-sales and design? (saving strategies and rightsizing recommendations are critical).

Yes, you’ll have a dedicated AWS account manager, plus access to technical SMEs and pre-sales engineering resources.

Additionally, our ServiceSolv team provides engineering, integration, deployment, and remediation services which enables you to offer end-to-end customer lifecycle support without building these functions internally. We can also provide white-labeled resources delivered under your branding, where applicable.

From: Sankar, Rupesh <Rupesh.Sankar@tdsynnex.com>
Sent: March 12, 2026 2:14 PM
To: Matthew Carter <Matthew.Carter@aptum.com>; Mohammadi, Maz <Mazyar.Mohammadi@tdsynnex.com>; Tammo, Gina <Gina.Tammo@tdsynnex.com>; Marc Pare <Marc.Pare@aptum.com>; Adam Nelson <Adam.Nelson@aptum.com>; Thomson, Christine <Christine.Thomson@tdsynnex.com>
Subject: RE: Placeholder Event: Preview of the new program through TD SYNNEX along with all the new incentives

Hi Matthew,

Please find the minutes from today’s call below.

***These are AI-generated minutes based on the discussion. Please review for accuracy.

Meeting Summary

AWS Partner Program Changes (Effective April 1st)

The meeting focused on major AWS partner program changes being rolled out, with Gina (TD SYNNEX subject matter expert) presenting a comprehensive overview. Key changes include a new base discount structure, deprecation of several legacy incentives, and new operational requirements. The changes represent a significant overhaul of how AWS incentivizes channel partners, with many updates piling up simultaneously affecting TD SYNNEX's ~4,900 global partners.

Timeline of Changes:
- January 1st: PGI started; PGD and other incentives deprecated
- February 1st: All RPPA changes went into effect
- April 1st: New base benefit, deprecation of technology component incentive, new NCI, deprecation of POD, PSD, and CEI incentives

Partner-Based Discount (Primary Focus)

The new 8% flat front-end discount replaces the previous 3% base + 4% technical capability discount structure. This discount applies to non-PPA, non-internal spend, excluding existing accounts and marketplace revenue. TD SYNNEX passes the full discount through to partners — they do not retain any portion.

Status Details:
- New Base Discount: 8% (up from 3% base; previously 3% + 4% technical competency = 7%)
- Existing Accounts (formerly "Shareshift"): Only 2% discount applies to any member account spending >$50,000/month prior to linking
- Existing Account Policy: Currently permanent (no time limit); TD SYNNEX has lobbied AWS to reduce to 1–2 years but AWS has not agreed
- First billing reflection: May bill (for April usage) will be the first to reflect the new 8% discount
- Competency benefits: Financial discount for competency is now absorbed into base rate, but competencies still provide lead generation and increased MDF access

Existing Accounts / Shareshift Classification

The $50,000 threshold applies at the individual member account level, not the customer or payer account as a whole. Strategies to address existing account classification were discussed.

Status Details:
- Accounts under $49,999/month at time of linking receive full channel discount
- Accounts at $50,000+ receive only 2% (existing account rate)
- Exception process available (business use case submission), but Gina noted ~80% failure rate
- Acceptable exception reasons: prior reseller went out of business, unique services the new reseller provides that the old one cannot
- Workarounds discussed:
- Modernization into new member accounts (acceptable)
- Pure lift-and-shift to new member account (frowned upon)
- Normalizing spend across member accounts before linking
- Specific Optum fintech customer noted: Has been consuming direct; team to explore business case exception with AWS to classify as base account given Optum's involvement since inception

DSA Migration & Timing (Moving to TD SYNNEX)

Optum is planning to move their US legal entity first, with Canada and UK as a secondary phase. The migration process and timing requirements were clarified.

Status Details:
- Process: Submit APN ID + DSA move request by region to AWS; Rupesh to provide email address
- Deadline: DSA move and CTA must be completed before end-user reporting closes (last Friday of the month — approximately March 22–23rd for April 1st effective date)
- Consent to Assign (CTA): TD SYNNEX will prepare a consolidated document with all payer IDs; Optum signs once; then a 10–15 minute call with both distributors' operations teams to transfer root access
- Important: If DSA is not moved in time, neither old nor new distributor receives the discount (street price applies)
- Canada: Rupesh remains account manager for both US and Canada regions; billing currency can be USD or CAD based on preference
- Former aggregator behavior: May attempt to retain the partner; TD SYNNEX noted competitors have dragged their feet in the past

New Customer Incentive (NCI)

NCI is a new program replacing POD (Partner Originated Discount), offering an additional 4% discount for 3 years on qualifying new accounts.

Status Details:
- Eligibility: Customer must have spent less than $50,000 in the prior 12 months (~$5,000/month) before linking
- Benefit: Extra 4% discount for 3 years (vs. POD's 5% for 2 years)
- Requirement: Customer must be enrolled in Billing Transfer
- Billing Transfer overview:
- Customer retains root access
- Handshake model (similar to Microsoft Azure CSP)
- Customer cannot see partner or distributor discounts
- Removes consent-to-assign requirement
- Reduces operational complexity and customer hesitation around giving up root

Bad Debt / Bankruptcy Protection

Optum raised concerns about customer bankruptcies and recovery options.

Status Details:
- Billing Transfer advantage: Partner can sever the handshake, stopping future liability; AWS holds remaining spend
- Infrastructure as a service is classified as a utility globally — cannot be shut off during bankruptcy protection
- TD SYNNEX process: Documents all spend, identifies legal counsel, petitions AWS for reimbursement on behalf of partners
- TD SYNNEX pays AWS every two weeks regardless of partner/customer payment status
- TD SYNNEX has successfully petitioned AWS to absorb losses in past cases
- TD SYNNEX noted as better positioned to absorb bad debt than individual partners

MDF (Market Development Funds)

TD SYNNEX offers an MDF match program tied to AWS MDF allocations.

Status Details:
- AWS MDF: Advanced partners with competency can access up to $50,000/year (typically ~$25,000); AWS covers 50% after proof of performance
- TD SYNNEX Match: Matches what AWS contributes (covering the other 50%)
- Allocation: Annual basis, not quarterly/monthly; based on qualification level and partner activity
- One-off events: Partners can request ad hoc MDF participation with a business case (ROI, cost, timing)
- TD SYNNEX distributes limited AWS funding across ~1,300–1,400 North America partners; more active partners get more access

Payment Terms

Payment terms are determined individually based on credit rating and financials.

Status Details:
- Process: TD SYNNEX credit team reviews financials during onboarding to establish credit line and terms
- Typical terms: Net 30 standard; Net 45 likely achievable; Net 90 rare (requires ~$2–4M/month in AWS spend)
- Terms cannot be confirmed in advance — financial review required first
- Christine to follow up offline on credit/payment term specifics

✅ Decisions Made

DSA Migration Scope: US legal entity first; Canada and UK as secondary phase

Billing Currency: US dollars preferred for current migration

Deck Sharing: Gina to share the comprehensive partner-facing program deck with the Optum/Matthew team

🎯 Action Items

@Rupesh
- Provide AWS email address for DSA move request submission
- Prepare consolidated Consent to Assign (CTA) document with all payer IDs
- Schedule operations call with former aggregator for root access transfer
- Follow up on Adam's questions submitted via chat

@Gina
- Share comprehensive partner-facing program deck (covering all PPA, NCI, support plan changes)
- Support business case exception request for Optum's fintech customer (direct-consuming client) to be classified as base account

@Christine
- Follow up offline on payment terms and credit line process for Optum
- Address Adam's questions from the chat via email

@Matthew / @Adam / @Marc (Optum Team)
- Initiate DSA move request for US region (send APN ID + request to move DSA from current distributor to TD SYNNEX)
- Complete CTA signing
- Evaluate fintech customer migration strategy (business case exception vs. modernization approach)
- Provide financials to TD SYNNEX credit team for payment terms assessment
- Review email sent by Marc on Tuesday covering payment terms and MDF details

📊 Updates Shared

TD SYNNEX Team Structure: Rupesh = primary account manager (North America); Gina = programs SME (AWS incentives, PPA, partner-led support); Christine = escalation; Maz = supervisor/escalation; pre-sales technical resource also assigned

Gina's availability note: High demand across many partners; Rupesh coordinates SME engagement as needed

📋 Additional Notes

The technical competency discount (previously 4%) has been absorbed into the new 8% base rate — no additional financial discount for holding a competency, though other benefits (leads, MDF access) remain

SPP certification no longer provides an incremental financial discount under the new structure

Billing Transfer is a strategic tool to reduce customer friction around root access and simplifies operational onboarding

All program changes represent a significant simultaneous shift — partners are encouraged to act quickly ahead of April 1st to avoid billing disruptions

Thanks & Regards

Rupesh Sankar

Business Development Manager

TD SYNNEX

6911 Creditview Road

Mississauga, ON, L5N 8G1

647 807-8277

Hi Charles,

Yes, the Software Subscription comes with Software Assurance which allows you to run both passive nodes with automatic failover and a DR node with manual failure without an additional license, so long as the passive nodes have the same core count.

This diagram is from SQL Server 2019 but explains it well:

The passive nodes must not be used for anything other than failover, and each passive node cannot have a larger core count that you have licenses. If the nodes are completely passive, and allow no SQL connections at all, then you only need to license the Active SQL server cores, in your case, just one four core server if you are in a Basic Availability Group and the second node is passive.

Lastly, I wanted to ensure you don’t have an appetite for Azure Arc, we mentioned it earlier and you didn’t seem to have any interest, correct?

Hi Charles,

SA is required to have the mobility rights to be able to use the VM on multiple hosts and just license the VM cores. Without SA you must license every core in every host.

Here’s your options:

SQL Server 2025 Standard - 2 core License Pack - 1 year @ $2,638.00 = x 2 for 4 cores = $5,276.00 x 2 servers = $10,552.00 (upfront payment per year)

SQL Server 2025 Standard - 2 core License Pack - 3 year @ $6,626.00 = x 2 for 4 cores = $13,252.00 x 2 servers = $26,504.00 (yearly payment for 3 years) = $8,834.67 per year

You'll need to purchase additional licenses if you upgrade the cores (and can't cancel mid-term for any kind of refund on either 1 or 3-year even if you are paying yearly). Price is locked in for the term once you make the purchase and even if MS increase the price you’re protected until your renewal date.

(N.B.: a 3-year paid annually does not incur the 5% surcharge).

Hi Charles,

Subject: RE: Request for SOC 2 Type II Report and ISO 27001 Certificate - Vendor Compliance Documentation Unfortunately, perpetual licenses are intended for physical servers and are not suitable for virtual environments.

Subscription licenses are purpose-built for virtual servers and can be licensed per virtual machine core. They include upgrade rights for SQL Server and can be used for DR or Azure scenarios. These licenses are typically paid annually upfront; however, Microsoft allows monthly payments with a 5% surcharge.

Available subscription options include:

1-year term paid upfront

1-year term paid monthly 5% surcharge

3-year term paid upfront

3-year term paid annually (likely without 5% surcharge, pending confirmation)

Alternatively, licensing can be consumed on an hourly basis through Azure Arc. While this can be more cost-effective than the highest-priced monthly subscription option, pricing is not fixed under this model and may increase during the term.

We can confirm final pricing structures and surcharge applicability based on your preferred term.

Hi Charles,

Great to meet you and thanks for reaching out.

Microsoft SQL licensing offers 2 options:

Subscription (with software assurance) - Paid annually, higher cost, but includes upgrade rights, Azure mobility, disaster recovery rights, and shared platform use. Available per core (sold in 2-core packs) for Standard or Enterprise edition, with monthly billing on a 1-year term.

Perpetual License – One-time upfront cost, lower price, but no upgrade or mobility rights. You’re licensed for a specific version and must use it on dedicated hardware. This can be licensed either:

Per server + user/device CALs (lower cost, least flexible), or

Per core (no CALS required).

Could you confirm:

Which edition you require (Standard or Enterprise)?

Whether the server is physical/dedicated or shared?

Which licensing model you'd like pricing for?

If you're running 2 servers with two cores each, I generally recommend per core licensing for simplicity and flexibility.

As part of our vendor management program, in-scope vendors are required to be reviewed from a security and compliance perspective. This is for your vendor – PagerDuty.

Although out of scope of our compliance program requirements, I would encourage you to conduct a full annual business review (financials, agreement, SLAs, etc.) of this vendor. If you decide to do so, please engage @Nedim Deovic and he can assist you.

In addition, @Fred Taylor-Young is also available to discuss any specific security issues that may be present in Aptum’s use of PagerDuty. If there are any, please feel free to add it to the comments in the report.

Subject: RE: Request for SOC 2 Type II Report and ISO 27001 Certificate - Vendor Compliance Documentation

Hi Charles,

Yes, the Software Subscription comes with Software Assurance which allows you to run both passive nodes with automatic failover and a DR node with manual failure without an additional license, so long as the passive nodes have the same core count.

This diagram is from SQL Server 2019 but explains it well:

The passive nodes must not be used for anything other than failover, and each passive node cannot have a larger core count that you have licenses. If the nodes are completely passive, and allow no SQL connections at all, then you only need to license the Active SQL server cores, in your case, just one four core server if you are in a Basic Availability Group and the second node is passive.

Lastly, I wanted to ensure you don’t have an appetite for Azure Arc, we mentioned it earlier and you didn’t seem to have any interest, correct?

Matthew Carter

VP, Project Management & Global Supply Chain

+1 (416) 847-0852
aptum.com | LinkedIn

Hi! Yes, we’re almost there, but wondering about one thing..

With Basic Availability Groups we have on SQL Server Standard, the secondary VM:

Cannot accept user queries

Cannot be used for reporting

Cannot offload workloads

Cannot be used for BI/dashboards

So by design, it seems if we read correctly, it fits Microsoft’s definition of a Passive Failover OSE

Can we thus save 50% of the costs?

Subject: RE: Request for SOC 2 Type II Report and ISO 27001 Certificate - Vendor Compliance Documentation

Hi Charles,

SA is required to have the mobility rights to be able to use the VM on multiple hosts and just license the VM cores. Without SA you must license every core in every host.

Here’s your options:

SQL Server 2025 Standard - 2 core License Pack - 1 year @ $2,638.00 = x 2 for 4 cores = $5,276.00 x 2 servers = $10,552.00 (upfront payment per year)

SQL Server 2025 Standard - 2 core License Pack - 3 year @ $6,626.00 = x 2 for 4 cores = $13,252.00 x 2 servers = $26,504.00 (yearly payment for 3 years) = $8,834.67 per year

You'll need to purchase additional licenses if you upgrade the cores (and can't cancel mid-term for any kind of refund on either 1 or 3-year even if you are paying yearly). Price is locked in for the term once you make the purchase and even if MS increase the price you’re protected until your renewal date.

(N.B.: a 3-year paid annually does not incur the 5% surcharge).

Matthew Carter

VP, Project Management & Global Supply Chain

From: Matthew Carter <Matthew.Carter@aptum.com>
Date: Friday, February 13, 2026 at 4:15 PM
To: Charles Louis Guerin <cguerin@obds.com>
Cc: Melvin Ching <Melvin.Ching@aptum.com>, Martin Tessier <Martin.Tessier@aptum.com>, Nedim Deovic <Nedim.Deovic@aptum.com>, Noe Ibarra <Noe.Ibarra@aptum.com>, Andrei Ianouchkevitch <Andrei.Ianouchkevitch@aptum.com>, Carlton Currey <Carlton.Currey@aptum.com>, Ian Rae <Ian.Rae@aptum.com>, Erik Tanguay <Erik.Tanguay@aptum.com>, Steve Rioux <Steve.Rioux@aptum.com>, Andy Petterson <Andy.Petterson@aptum.com>

Hi Charles,

Subject: RE: Request for SOC 2 Type II Report and ISO 27001 Certificate - Vendor Compliance Documentation Unfortunately, perpetual licenses are intended for physical servers and are not suitable for virtual environments.

Subscription licenses are purpose-built for virtual servers and can be licensed per virtual machine core. They include upgrade rights for SQL Server and can be used for DR or Azure scenarios. These licenses are typically paid annually upfront; however, Microsoft allows monthly payments with a 5% surcharge.

Available subscription options include:

1-year term paid upfront

1-year term paid monthly 5% surcharge

3-year term paid upfront

3-year term paid annually (likely without 5% surcharge, pending confirmation)

Alternatively, licensing can be consumed on an hourly basis through Azure Arc. While this can be more cost-effective than the highest-priced monthly subscription option, pricing is not fixed under this model and may increase during the term.

We can confirm final pricing structures and surcharge applicability based on your preferred term.

Matthew Carter

VP, Project Management & Global Supply Chain

+1 (416) 847-0852

Subject: RE: Request for SOC 2 Type II Report and ISO 27001 Certificate - Vendor Compliance Documentation

Hi Charles,

Great to meet you and thanks for reaching out.

Microsoft SQL licensing offers 2 options:

Subscription (with software assurance) - Paid annually, higher cost, but includes upgrade rights, Azure mobility, disaster recovery rights, and shared platform use. Available per core (sold in 2-core packs) for Standard or Enterprise edition, with monthly billing on a 1-year term.

Perpetual License – One-time upfront cost, lower price, but no upgrade or mobility rights. You’re licensed for a specific version and must use it on dedicated hardware. This can be licensed either:

Per server + user/device CALs (lower cost, least flexible), or

Per core (no CALS required).

Could you confirm:

Which edition you require (Standard or Enterprise)?

Whether the server is physical/dedicated or shared?

Which licensing model you'd like pricing for?

If you're running 2 servers with two cores each, I generally recommend per core licensing for simplicity and flexibility.

Matthew Carter

VP, Project Management & Global Supply Chain

Dear Aptum Compliance Team,

As part of our organization's ISO 27001 compliance requirements, we are required to maintain documented evidence of our critical vendors' security controls and certifications. Toronto

We understand that SOC reports and certain compliance documents are confidential. We are prepared to execute a mutual Non-Disclosure Agreement (NDA) if required prior to receiving these documents. Please send us your standard NDA template, or let us know if you would prefer we provide ours.
